cases.description: Fractional-N Divider Accumulator Flow: keeps all outputs at the low rail so there is no DCO, feedback, lock, or control behavior.
Fractional-N Divider Accumulator Flow: generates the clocks but drives lock high continuously so the disturbance and reacquire behavior is missing.
Fractional-N Divider Accumulator Flow: uses the wrong base integer divider so the late-window feedback cadence cannot track the reference.
Fractional-N Divider Accumulator Flow: keeps the control voltage fixed instead of correcting after the reference frequency step.
Fractional-N Divider Accumulator Flow: reports a control-voltage monitor outside the supply rails while otherwise preserving most clock behavior.
